verdict_rationale: The spike was executed rigorously and documented honestly. Scope discipline is sound (only spikes/, findings/, and the expected doc/ areas touched; no src/, tests/, or .github/). The anti-over-mocking guardrail held (real mermaid.render() against real happy-dom). The H4 FAIL (0/5) is the designed, informative output, recorded with root cause and ecosystem corroboration. All findings are minor/nit and do not affect the spike's conclusion. Reproducibility was independently verified: probe:all re-run produced byte-identical goldens (incl. the Rule-5 gantt today-line fix) and the exact digests cited in the findings doc. DoR findings F1-F4 were all addressed in lifecycle phase-7 doc reconciliation. The CEO-level open decisions (ADR-0001 reconsideration, MS2-E4-S1 re-scoping, possible follow-up shim spike) are correctly deferred and not auto-decided.

findings.title: H2 process-detection regex under-matches canonical chrome/chromium process names
findings.description: chromeRe = /\b(chrom|headless_shell|google-chrome|chromium-browser)\b/i does NOT match '/opt/google/chrome/chrome', '/usr/bin/chromium', '/snap/bin/chromium', or bare 'chrome --headless' (verified empirically: \bchrom\b needs a word boundary after 'm', but 'chrome' has 'e' there). This weakens the NFR-DEP-2 process-delta arm: a real chromium spawn could go undetected, so the 'two independent checks' design is overstated for that arm. The H2 PASS verdict itself is sound because (a) the dependency-tree check (NFR-DEP-1) is the load-bearing half and uses robust substring matching against the transitive tree (0 forbidden), and (b) Mermaid under happy-dom is pure-JS and spawns no process by construction. The findings doc's claim '2 pre-existing chrome processes on the host' is reported via this insensitive regex.
findings.suggested_fix: Broaden the process regex to a substring match consistent with the dep-tree check, e.g. /chrom(?:e|ium)?/i or /(\bchrom(e|ium)?\b|headless_shell)/i. Add a one-line note in findings section 3 (H2) acknowledging the process-regex limitation and stating that NFR-DEP-1 (transitive dep tree) is the primary evidence for E4-S1's stronger check.

findings.title: Rule 5 defensive regex over-matches any class containing the substring 'today'
findings.description: The second defensive regex /<[^>]*class="[^"]*today[^"]*"[^>]*>(<\/[^>]*>)?/g matches e.g. class="today-special", broader than the comment ('any element carrying a today class') implies. Operates only on the digest form and works for current Mermaid gantt output (which emits exactly class="today"); the load-bearing first regex /<g class="...today...">...</g>/ already strips the gantt today-group. Reproducibility verified: the committed gantt golden is byte-identical after a re-run on a different wall-clock date.
findings.suggested_fix: Either tighten to class="today" exact, or drop the defensive regex and rely on the first one; either way document the over-match risk for MS2-E4-S1 (the normalizer is documented 'lift verbatim').

findings.title: WORKSPACE_ROOT computed via fragile string-replace rather than path.join
findings.description: WORKSPACE_ROOT = import.meta.dir.replace("/probes", "") breaks if any path segment contains '/probes' or on non-Linux path separators. chromium-absence.ts:25 uses the robust join(import.meta.dir, "..") for the same purpose; the two probes are inconsistent.
findings.suggested_fix: Use join(import.meta.dir, "..") for consistency and portability.

findings.title: verbatimModuleSyntax relaxed to false without an inline justification
findings.description: The typescript rules mark verbatimModuleSyntax:true as non-negotiable for the main project; the spike relaxes it to false. The plan said 'relax the minimum necessary flag and document the reason in a comment.' The tsconfig header comment justifies the overall standalone posture but not this specific relaxation. The spike's imports contain no type-only cases that obviously require the relaxation.
findings.suggested_fix: Either set verbatimModuleSyntax:true (likely works given the current imports), or add an inline // reason: comment next to the flag explaining why it is relaxed for the spike.

findings.title: Duplicate open_questions: YAML key
findings.description: The working-tree pm-notes has 'open_questions: []' immediately followed by a second 'open_questions:' mapping with the real list. Most YAML parsers let the second shadow the first, so data is likely fine, but it is invalid YAML hygiene in a change-management artifact.
findings.suggested_fix: Delete the empty 'open_questions: []' line so only the populated mapping remains.

findings.title: Standalone tsc --noEmit cannot resolve 'bun' types (informational)
findings.description: tsconfig references "types": ["bun"] but the spike has no @types/bun/bun-types devDep, so a standalone `tsc --noEmit` fails with TS2688 'Cannot find type definition file for bun'. Runs fine under Bun (ambient types). The plan requires execution via `bun run` (not tsc) and defines no typecheck script, so this is not a defect; flagged only so it is not a surprise if a typecheck step is ever added.
findings.suggested_fix: No action required for the spike. If a typecheck is desired later, add @types/bun as a devDep or document that typechecking is Bun-runtime-only.

AC1.evidence: findings sec 2/3 (H1); probes/determinism.ts; fixtures/flowchart.expected.svg + gantt.expected.svg. Reproduced: digests 88a55f72...52f887 (flowchart), cd94ccce...630d9 (gantt) match findings doc exactly. 2/5 render & byte-stable; 3/5 fail-to-render (same root cause as H4), recorded honestly.

AC2.evidence: findings sec 3 (H2); probes/chromium-absence.ts. Reproduced: bun pm ls --all transitive (117 lines, 0 forbidden); process delta 0. Caveat: process-detection regex under-matches canonical chrome (finding 1); dep-tree check is sound and load-bearing.

AC3.evidence: findings sec 3 (H3); probes/run-all.ts. Reproduced: probe:all ran end-to-end on Bun 1.1.34 linux/x64, all 5 stages exit 0, no Node fallback.

AC4.evidence: findings sec 3 (H4); probes/fidelity.ts. Reproduced: 0/5; flowchart/gantt degenerate (no <svg> root, negative gantt widths); sequence/class/state throw with the exact messages recorded. Per task framing, FAIL-with-evidence satisfies this AC.

AC5.evidence: findings sec 3 (H5); probes/security.ts. Reproduced: securityLevel=strict active (read back); 0 <script>/onerror=/onload=/javascript: across 3 adversarial fixtures; scoped per DoR F2 (default-config XSS/script only; full SVG sanitization deferred to MS2-E4-S1).

AC6.evidence: findings/mermaid-render-spike-findings.md: explicit PASS/FAIL per H1-H5, evidence pointers, single MS-0002 recommendation (sec 10), normalization rules (sec 5), golden-pair list (sec 6), catastrophic-failure assessment (sec 1).

AC7.evidence: findings sec 7; scripts/secret-scan.sh. Reproduced: 0 secrets in committed artifacts.

Phase 1 (fixtures).note: bonus 3rd adversarial fixture (adversarial-external-ref.mmd) per DoR F2

Phase 3 (determinism + normalizer).note: post-phase correction d80a1ea: Rule 5 strips gantt today-line (cross-run drift fix)

lifecycle phase 7 (doc-sync).note: ADR-0001/0002, TDR-0004, feature spec, both stories, 00-index reconciled

dor_findings_addressed.F1: YES - ADR-0002 stop-criterion #1 reworded to same-OS-byte-stable + cross-OS-hash-stable via per-OS cache key (ADR-0002 line 217); C-1 clarified to logical-input hashing not raw byte equality (lines 85-90); actual cross-OS result recorded in Constraint Compliance Attestation (same-OS only).
dor_findings_addressed.F2: YES - H5 scope note in findings sec 11/13 (default-config XSS/script only; full SVG sanitization deferred to MS2-E4-S1 SVGSanitizer); optional 3rd adversarial fixture added and rendered.
dor_findings_addressed.F3: YES - chromium-absence probe uses bun pm ls --all (transitive); 117 lines, 0 forbidden. Recorded in findings sec 3 (H2).
dor_findings_addressed.F4: YES - ADR-0002 and TDR-0004 frontmatter (Accepted) vs body (Proposed) mismatch reconciled; bodies now explicitly state governance-Accepted and distinguish from substantive Part B state.

reproducibility_verification.method: Ran `bun run probe:all` (and individual probes) in spikes/mermaid-render on Bun 1.1.34 linux/x64.
reproducibility_verification.results: All 5 stages exit 0. H1 digests match findings doc exactly (flowchart 88a55f72..., gantt cd94ccce...). H2 dep-tree 117 lines / 0 forbidden, process delta 0. H4 0/5 with exact error messages. H5 all counts zero, strict active. AC7 0 secrets. The determinism probe rewrites fixtures/*.expected.svg; after re-run `git diff --stat fixtures/` is empty = goldens byte-identical (Rule-5 gantt today-line fix confirmed reproducible across a different wall-clock date).

surfaced_decisions_needing_human_input: None beyond the already-flagged CEO items (ADR-0001 fundamental reconsideration; MS2-E4-S1 re-scoping; possible follow-up svgdom/getBBox-shim spike). These are correctly captured in pm-notes open_questions and findings sec 4/10, and correctly NOT auto-decided by the doc-sync.
next_step: PROCEED. Optional cleanup of the 1 minor + 5 nit findings can be folded into a single chore(spike) commit before PR creation if desired, but is not required to pass review.
